sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés has shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, indicating a relatively stable form with a focus on defense (under 2.5 goals in 9/9 matches). Osasuna, on the other hand, is in better shape with an unbeaten streak of 3 matches (2 wins, 1 draw), demonstrating attacking potential, especially through players like Ante Budimir and Rubén García.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Analysis
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, matches between Alavés and Osasuna have been competitive, with one draw (2-2) and one Alavés win (2-1). Both games saw goals from both sides, suggesting a tendency for open, scoring affairs when these teams meet.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Osasuna holds a better league position (8th, 51 points) compared to Alavés (15th, 41 points), with a stronger attacking output (47 goals scored vs 37). Alavés excels in creating penalty area entries (2nd in league), but struggles with possession and finishing, while Osasuna often scores early and controls games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Alavés relies on Kike García for goals (13 scored), while Nahuel Tenaglia has been in form defensively. Osasuna’s attack is led by Budimir (21 goals) and supported by Rubén García’s creativity, which could exploit Alavés’ defensive vulnerabilities.
